title: Loss of the autoimmune risk gene TREX1 reveals a convergence of mechanisms promoting immune tolerance loss and antitumor immunity.
snippet: Checkpoint inhibitors targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4 have transformed cancer therapy. Both are genetically associated with autoimmune disorders. Moreover, certain immune-related adverse events and autoimmune risk variants are linked to the clinical efficacy of checkpoint inhibition. These associations suggest common principles governing successful cancer immunot...
